channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Shohei Ohtani and four other Japanese players come home to start the MLB season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — It's only two games to start the Major League Baseball season — the World Series-champion Dodgers against the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday and Wednesday at the Tokyo Dome. For the record, the MLB regular season consists of almost 2,500 games.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WADE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stephen Curry becomes first player in NBA history to make 4,000 3-pointers
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry's shooting range and accuracy from way back have long stood alone — whether with a defender or two in his face or when letting it fly from the logo.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bubble watch: Indiana and Xavier now feeling the heat with Texas and North Carolina closing strong
item.description: A look at the NCAA Tournament bubble picture: Missed opportunities Xavier and Indiana had chances to give their resumes a significant boost Thursday, but the Musketeers lost 89-87 to No. 25 Marquette, and Indiana fell 72-59 to No. 23 Oregon.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Heartbreak for DePaul as 1st trip to Big East semifinals slips away with late collapse at MSG
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — At long last, downtrodden DePaul was finally about to produce its own indelible moment at the Big East Tournament. A big upset was in the bag for the Blue Demons — and their first trip to the semifinals at Madison Square Garden. And then it slipped through their fingers.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hepburn's buzzer-beater lifts No. 13 Louisville to another big moment in Kelsey-led resurgence
item.description: Chucky Hepburn hit a shot at the buzzer to help No. 13 Louisville add another highlight to Pat Kelsey's successful first season with the Cardinals.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Caitlin Clark jokingly trolls Fever teammate Lexie Hull ahead of Unrivaled game
item.description: Caitlin Clark and Lexie Hull developed on-court chemistry during their first season together, but the Indiana Fever duo appear to be in sync even when they're not on the hardwood.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stanford out of ACC tournament on Louisville buzzer-beater in furious finish
item.description: The Cardinal erased an eight-point deficit in the final two minutes, but Louisville made the last bucket.
item.dc:creator: Mike Barber, The Mercury News

item.title: Injuries to freshman star Cooper Flagg, Maliq Brown hit at inopportune time for No. 1 Duke
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Cooper Flagg went airborne to snag a rebound in his first ACC Tournament game when Duke's freshman star landed awkwardly and crashed to the court, holding his left ankle and writhing in pain.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Right to Play Act aims to lift IHSA restrictions on high school athletes in club sports
item.description: Illinois State Rep. Janet Yang-Rohr has introduced House Bill 3037, the "Right to Play Act," which would eliminate the IHSA rules that restrict student-athletes from playing their sport during the high school season, potentially improving opportunities for college scholarships.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rebik, WGN 9

item.title: The number of NHL players still playing without face visors is dwindling, only a handful left
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Ryan O’Reilly has had the conversation a number of times. Family members will often call or text to tell the veteran NHL center again that it is time to put on a visor. Sticks have nicked O’Reilly’s face. Pucks and skate blades have come perilously close to his unprotected eyes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohtani's Tokyo airport arrival hidden from fans hoping to catch a glimpse of Japanese star
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Hundreds of fans at Tokyo’s Haneda Airport, who lined up 10 deep and hoped to catch a glimpse of Shohei Ohtani as he arrived on Thursday, saw nothing of the Los Angeles Dodgers superstar.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WADE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mountour scores fastest OT goal in NHL history in Kraken's comeback win over Canadiens
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Brandon Mountour had the fastest goal in NHL overtime history, scoring his second goal of the game four seconds into the extra period to give the Seattle Kraken a 5-4 comeback victory over the Montreal Canadiens on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jazz give Grizzlies a scare, but lose seventh straight
item.description: MEMPHIS (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz are well on their way to their worst record in franchise history, but they're not going down without a fight. Luke Kennard scored 30 points and Ja Morant had 28, helping the Memphis Grizzlies top the Utah Jazz 122-115 on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Friendly Freshmen; Aztecs Basketball Pharaoh Compton and Taj DeGourville hold lifelong bond
item.description: Aztecs freshmen-duo Pharaoh Compton and Taj Degourville's friendship goes back longer than you think.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz fined $100,000 for not playing Markkanen
item.description: MEMPHIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA on Wednesday fined the Utah Jazz $100,000 for violating its player participation policy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Palisade girls soccer escapes Montrose in 2024 SWL title rematch
item.description: It was a battle between two of the top Western Slope girls soccer teams at Long Family Memorial Park.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Warriors' Steph Curry becomes investor in Unrivaled Basketball
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Stephen Curry has joined Unrivaled basketball as an investor. The Golden State Warriors guard is the latest sports star to join the 3v3 basketball league as an investor, joining the likes of the NBA's Giannis Antetokounmpo and Carmelo Anthony, and tennis star Coco Gauff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Connor Bedard scores in overtime to lift Blackhawks to 4-3 win over Utah
item.description: Connor Bedard scored 3:10 into overtime to give the Chicago Blackhawks a 4-3 victory over the Utah Hockey Club on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Raiders acquire quarterback Geno Smith from Seahawks for a 3rd-round pick, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders answered their most pressing personnel question of the offseason Friday by acquiring quarterback Geno Smith in a trade with Seattle and reuniting him with his former coach, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5
